div.p: To all who shall see or hear the present letters, H., abbot of Kelso, and the convent of the same place, wish eternal salvation in the Lord. All should know that whatever right we used to have in the lands and pastures of Mow within the portion of Walter son of Alan, the Steward of Scotland, and also the common pasture that we used to have in the moor of Innerwick, we have resigned and quitclaimed for ever to the same Walter and his heirs or their attorneys, nothing of the right in the same to be claimed back in the future; in return for a portion of the said moor of Innerwick conferred by the same Walter to us as an exchange. As testimony of this matter we have applied the seal of our chapter to the present document. With these witnesses: the lord William, bishop of Glasgow and Chancellor of Scotland; the lord William, abbot of Paisley; Master William Lindsay, dean of Glasgow; Master Hugh, archdeacon of Glasgow; Master William Greenlaw, and many others. Enacted at Kelso in the year of grace One Thousand Two Hundred and Thirty Six.
